 {"id":"bd-1xhje","title":"Inline present cleanup on I/O error","description":"TerminalWriter::present_inline uses ? on writes; on IO error it returns before restoring cursor/sync/scroll-region, leaving the terminal in a bad state if the runtime keeps going. Add best-effort cleanup on error.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseBeacon","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:38.633291975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:06:18.594195276Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:06:18.594177763Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: present_inline wraps errors and calls best_effort_inline_cleanup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1xo","title":"Windows Terminal compatibility layer","description":"PHASE 7 - POLISH: WINDOWS SUPPORT

## Background
Windows Terminal v1 support is deferred but must have clear integration path. Modern Windows Terminal supports ANSI, older conhost needs different handling.

## Technical Requirements (v1 Scope - Windows Terminal Only)
- Detect Windows Terminal vs legacy conhost
- Enable VT processing mode via SetConsoleMode
- Handle Windows-specific escape sequences
- PTY spawning via ConPTY API
- Signal handling differences (Ctrl+C, Ctrl+Break)
- Path handling for Windows conventions

## Out of Scope for v1
- Legacy conhost support (Windows 7/8)
- PowerShell-specific handling
- Windows-specific widgets

## Design Rationale
Per ADR-004, Windows v1 targets modern Windows Terminal only. This dramatically simplifies implementation while covering majority of Windows developer use.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Windows Terminal detection
- [ ] VT mode enabling
- [ ] ConPTY integration for PTY tests
- [ ] Signal handling shim
- [ ] CI testing on Windows
- [ ] Feature gate: windows

## Testing
- GitHub Actions Windows runner
- Manual testing on Windows Terminal
- Document known limitations

## Location: ftui-render crate + ftui-pty crate (platform-specific code)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:20:16.533908639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:01:10.730619535Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:01:10.730598736Z","close_reason":"Windows Terminal detection: treat WT_SESSION as non-dumb even when TERM is empty; added unit test + docs update.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xo","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1xo","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

triage.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyBeaver","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-11T05:34:26.842109688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T03:09:40.644311598Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hotspots","pane","perf","profiling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-23pre","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-2bav7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-2dy6m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-2eqqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-3jmx7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1057,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Profiling conclusion summary for the next optimization phase: the pane-core replay path is not a good candidate for more micro-optimizations alone. The high-EV opportunities are architectural: (1) stop replaying from the baseline for deep histories, (2) stop paying whole-tree clone/validate costs for local operations, (3) improve profiling fidelity with longer-lived harnesses and symbolized artifacts, and (4) make memory retention explicit and bounded for checkpointing/persistent structures. The new child/dependent bead graph created from this comment is intended to be fully self-contained so future sessions can work directly from Beads without consulting any external optimization memo.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:33:45Z"},{"id":1082,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Progress update: started a profiling-evidence cleanup slice. `scripts/pane_profile.sh` now emits `symbol_metadata.txt` for the three pane bench binaries, recording binary paths plus debug-info readiness, and `docs/profiling/bd-1y0ph-pane-hotspot-capture.md` has been updated so the note no longer incorrectly claims the bench binaries are still stripped. This tightens the profiling bundle itself so future `perf report` work can trust symbol-readiness from captured artifacts rather than assumption.","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:44:07Z"},{"id":1083,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Progress update: reran `./scripts/pane_profile.sh --test` after fixing the symbol-metadata path selection bug. Current artifact truth is now explicit: `layout_bench` reports `binary_source=executed` and `debug_info=present`, while `pane_terminal_bench` and `pane_pointer_bench` report `binary_source=executed` but `local_binary_status=missing` after `rch` artifact retrieval. This means the profiling bundle is now honest about what can and cannot support local `perf report` stack attribution; the remaining blocker for fully symbolized multi-surface profiling is preserving or retrieving the exact runtime/web bench binaries locally.","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:33:29Z"},{"id":1098,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","author":"WindyBeaver","text":"Progress update: fixed the profiling runner's RCH exact-binary trust gap. Root cause was that symbol artifact fetches used the raw endpoint printed by rch (for example ubuntu@192.168.1.124), which lacks the SSH identity/options configured on the worker alias. The runner now parses the selected worker alias for scp fetches, records the endpoint separately as worker_endpoint, and treats a missing local target/release/deps directory as an empty candidate set instead of emitting noisy find errors. Verified with ./scripts/pane_profile.sh --test --out-dir target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph-windybeaver: all four pane surfaces report binary_source=executed_remote_fetched, exact_binary_status=available, fetch_error=none, and debug_info=present.","created_at":"2026-04-25T03:09:40Z"}]}

downstream optimization beads can use the documented opportunity matrix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hotspots","pane","perf","profiling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-23pre","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-2bav7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-2dy6m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-2eqqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-3jmx7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1057,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Profiling conclusion summary for the next optimization phase: the pane-core replay path is not a good candidate for more micro-optimizations alone.  {"id":"bd-1y0ph.1","title":"task(pane-perf): preserve exact executed pane bench binaries and symbol manifests across rch profiling runs","description":"Goal
- Make pane profiling artifact bundles authoritative by preserving or explicitly materializing the exact bench binaries executed under `rch`, along with auditable symbol/readiness metadata for each pane surface.

Why this exists
- Current profiling evidence is now honest enough to show the real gap: for `ftui-runtime` and `ftui-web`, the exact executed bench binary path is known but the binary is not available locally after `rch` artifact retrieval. That prevents trustworthy local `perf report` stack attribution on those surfaces.

Scope
- Determine why runtime/web bench binaries are not locally present after `rch` retrieval while the layout bench is.
- Fix the profiling workflow so exact executed binaries or equivalent symbol-bearing artifacts are preserved locally.
- Keep the artifact contract explicit: path, origin, symbol status, and trust level per surface.
- Preserve the current honesty guarantees so missing artifacts are never silently replaced by stale local guesses.

Deliverables
- Stable exact-binary retention or retrieval path for pane benches under `rch`.
- Updated profiling runner/artifact contract documenting binary provenance.
- Regression checks that fail if exact-binary preservation silently regresses.
- Notes on any remaining worker/local-path constraints.

Acceptance
- Pane profiling bundles can prove which exact binaries were executed and whether they support local symbolized stack attribution.
- Runtime/web surfaces stop relying on ambiguous local fallback binaries.
- Profiling trust boundaries remain explicit and machine-checkable.","acceptance_criteria":"1) The profiling workflow preserves or explicitly materializes the exact executed pane bench binaries, or else records a machine-checkable reason why they are unavailable locally.
2) Regression checks fail if symbol manifests, binary provenance, or trust annotations silently degrade.
3) The artifact bundle includes enough metadata and logging that future sessions can tell exactly which pane surfaces support trustworthy local stack attribution.
4) The solution works with `rch` rather than bypassing it, and any remaining worker/local-artifact constraints are documented for operators and developers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:00:55.773652886Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-15T15:59:49.719011410Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T15:59:49.718572309Z","close_reason":"Completed: exact executed pane bench binaries preserved under target/pane-profiling/bd-1y0ph/executed-binaries with symbol provenance in symbol_metadata.txt","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pane","perf","profiling","rch","symbols"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1y0ph","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:00:55.773652886Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1085,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph.1","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Reasoning: this bead exists because the profiling lane hit an observability trust bottleneck, not just a compute bottleneck. If exact executed binaries are not preserved across `rch` runs, stack-level evidence for runtime/web benches stays partially speculative. Future work should treat artifact provenance as part of profiling correctness, not just tooling polish.","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1y0ph.2","title":"task(pane-perf): capture trustworthy multi-surface call-stack profiles and finalize hotspot opportunity matrix","description":"Goal
- Complete the pane profiling lane by collecting trustworthy stack-attribution evidence for core, terminal, and web surfaces and consolidating it into the final hotspot opportunity matrix that should drive the next optimization beads.

Why this exists
- Counter-based profiling already shows that pane-core is compute-dense while the terminal/web adapters are branch-heavy, but the stack-quality story is still incomplete. This bead owns the final trustworthy profiling synthesis rather than leaving future sessions to reconstruct conclusions from scattered artifacts.

Scope
- Capture trustworthy call-stack evidence for all three pane surfaces once exact-binary trust is established.
- Normalize results into one self-contained opportunity matrix with impact, confidence, effort, and proof-readiness.
- Separate pane-core asymptotic problems from terminal/web adapter-state-machine problems.
- Document which conclusions are strong, which remain uncertain, and what evidence quality each recommendation rests on.

Deliverables
- Multi-surface call-stack profiles/flamegraphs or equivalent stack reports.
- Final hotspot opportunity matrix with EV ranking.
- Explicit narrative for pane-core vs terminal vs web bottlenecks.
- Handoff artifact that justifies which optimization beads should go first.

Acceptance
- All pane surfaces have trustworthy stack-level or equivalent profiling evidence, with explicit trust annotations when symbols or exact binaries are missing.
- The final opportunity matrix is self-contained enough that future optimization sessions do not need to reconstruct the evidence chain from raw logs.
- The matrix clearly distinguishes asymptotic, branch-prediction, allocation, and artifact-trust problems.","acceptance_criteria":"1) All three pane surfaces have trustworthy stack-level or equivalent call-stack evidence, with explicit trust annotations when exact binaries or symbols are unavailable.
2) The final opportunity matrix ranks bottlenecks by impact, confidence, effort, and proof-readiness, and clearly separates pane-core asymptotic work from terminal/web adapter-state work.
3) The profiling handoff includes detailed logs, artifact paths, residual uncertainties, and clear next-step recommendations so future sessions do not need to reconstruct the evidence chain from scratch.
4) The matrix is good enough to justify downstream optimization order and to explain the user-facing tradeoffs of each optimization lane.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:03:00.084540798Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","updated_at":"2026-03-15T16:14:28.927099176Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T16:14:28.926588097Z","close_reason":"Completed: pane profiling now emits trustworthy multi-surface stack summaries and the final hotspot opportunity matrix is documented in docs/profiling/bd-1y0ph-pane-hotspot-capture.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["callstacks","flamegraph","pane","perf","profiling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1k7ek.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:27:40.213339208Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1y0ph","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:03:00.084540798Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1y0ph.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:04:29.507428778Z","created_by":"AmberBadger","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1087,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph.2","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Intent: this bead closes the profiling loop. The project now has enough evidence to know that pane-core, terminal, and web surfaces fail for different reasons; this work turns that into one trustworthy, self-contained opportunity matrix with confidence notes, residual uncertainty, and a justified optimization order.","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1y98h","title":"Mermaid Error Rendering + Recovery Panel","description":"Background
- Parse/layout errors must be surfaced without breaking the UI.

Scope
- Define structured error panel for Mermaid blocks (header + location + message).
- Include fallback raw code view with line numbers when in debug mode.
- Support partial-render overlay when a recoverable AST exists.
- Honor config error_mode (panel/raw/both) and log decisions to JSONL.
- Ensure errors are deterministic and logged to JSONL with codes.

Considerations
- Errors must not crash rendering or contaminate layout caches.
- Keep panel width-aware with proper wrapping.

Success Criteria
- Invalid Mermaid never crashes; a readable error panel is shown.
- Errors include line/column and hint when possible.
- Snapshot tests for error panel rendering.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:50:44.066534801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:30:14.175965777Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:30:14.175945789Z","close_reason":"Implemented error panel/overlay + raw view, added error codes + JSONL logging + snapshots","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1y98h","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y98h","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y98h","depends_on_id":"bd-pt0dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
